prompt: Develop a HarborFresh Offline Operations Console system for a seafood co-op that runs entirely on a single device without an internet connection, supporting member-facing group-buy ordering as well as staff operations for content, dispatch, training registration, and bookkeeping. The application is a responsive React Single Page Application with React Router navigation and complex local UI state (for example, a Campaign Detail page with Tabs, a Checkout Drawer, and a Dispatch Board with a draggable Kanban-style scheduling board). User roles include Administrator, Content Editor, Content Reviewer, Member, Dispatcher, Finance Clerk, and Instructor. Fish knowledge is managed as structured entries (taxonomy hierarchy down to species, morphology notes, habitat, distribution, protection level, and local media assets such as images/audio/video) with a draft–review–publish workflow; reviewers approve or reject with comments, and publishing can be scheduled (e.g., publish at 09:00 AM on 04/15/2026). The group-buy flow allows members to start or join a campaign with a defined minimum participant count and cutoff time; orders follow a strict status machine (Created → Confirmed → Fulfilled, with Cancelled/Refunded paths) where "payment" is recorded as an offline method (cash/card-on-pickup/manual mark) rather than processed, and unpaid orders auto-close after 30 minutes. Delivery planning generates delivery tasks and batches using local constraints (vehicle capacity in lbs, driver shift hours, priority, and promised pickup/delivery time windows); dispatchers can manually adjust assignments via board interactions, with immediate conflict detection and one-click recalculation. Course registration supports training rounds with MM/DD/YYYY time windows, prerequisites, capacity with waitlists, add/drop deadlines (e.g., drop allowed until 11:59 PM the day before), and an enrollment results view with change history. A notification center provides in-app messages only (no email/SMS), using templates for reminders (pickup due, hold available, overdue, fee changes), with an Inbox Table, read/unread filters, and visible send/failure logs plus local retry queues. All data processing runs in a frontend service layer and persists locally using IndexedDB as the primary "database," with optional LocalStorage for lightweight session flags and UI preferences; users can export/import the full dataset as an encrypted JSON file using browser Blob download/upload (no server sync). Versioned publishing stores each fish entry revision with author, timestamp, diff summary, and rollback within the last 50 versions. To prevent oversubscription and ensure idempotency for course enrollment and group-buy joins, each write operation carries a unique operationId, and updates use optimistic locking with a record version field enforced inside IndexedDB transactions. The dispatch decision log records every auto-plan and manual adjustment with a required reason (minimum 10 characters) and immutable timestamps. Income/expense/transfer entry uses validated forms (USD with two decimals, sales tax rate 0.00–12.00%, required chart-of-accounts code) and detects duplicate vouchers by hashing (SHA-256) payee + amount + date + memo; attachments are stored as Blobs with type/size checks (e.g., PDF/JPG/PNG up to 10 MB) and basic fingerprinting, while any "OCR results" are handled as user-imported text requiring manual review before posting. Security is local-only: username/password login with minimum 12 characters, salted PBKDF2 hashing, automatic lock after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes, role-based access control on every route and action, and field-level encryption (WebCrypto AES-GCM) for sensitive notes and financial account identifiers stored in IndexedDB.
